steps.title: Set off the trail detail
steps.content: The phrase "which is 4.36 miles long" gives extra information about the San Francisquito Trail. Because it interrupts the subject before the verb "have become," it must be set off with commas on both sides.

steps.title: Use paired commas
steps.content: A lacks the comma before the "which" clause and uses an unmatched dash. B wrongly uses a colon after "Trail." C opens the nonessential clause with a comma but fails to close it before the main verb. Paired commas correctly bracket the detail. The answer is D.
